articles.title: FIGMA RELEASES THE PATTERN LIBRARY
articles.summary: The Figma Pattern Library streamlines UI design with reusable, accessible, and customizable components. It enhances consistency, supports scalability, and integrates seamlessly within Figma. The library features accessibility-first design, detailed documentation, and real-time collaboration. Ideal for startups, enterprises, and freelancers, it helps save time and fosters high-quality, cohesive digital experiences.

articles.title: ADOBE'S NEW STUDENT DEAL IS PURE MARKETING GENIUS
SPOTIFY'S LATEST UI DESIGN CHANGE IS DRIVING PEOPLE CRAZY
articles.summary: Spotify recently introduced a ‘Create' button to facilitate playlist creation. The button replaced the traditional ‘Library' button, leading to user frustration. The change appears to be an attempt to promote Spotify's AI playlist feature, but many users find it disruptive to their navigation habits. The backlash highlights the importance of user-centered design and the risks of implementing features without considering user preferences.

articles.title: THE MAKING OF THE FIGMA PATTERN LIBRARY
articles.summary: The Figma Pattern Library (FPL) was rebuilt from the ground up to unify a fragmented design system. Using a collaborative design-engineering approach to bridge design intent and technical implementation, the Figma team leveraged automation and Figma's REST API for syncing design tokens and variables, achieving improved consistency, accessibility, and adoption across the system.

articles.title: THE 5 MOST DANGEROUS (AND EXCITING) IDEAS FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Embrace creativity, take risks, and recognize that good ideas can come from anyone, regardless of training or experience. Success in design involves persuasion, influence, and the courage to champion ideas while fostering collaboration across diverse perspectives.

articles.title: COGNITIVE BIASES IN UX DESIGN
articles.summary: Cognitive biases are mental shortcuts we use to simplify decision-making. Using these shortcuts can lead to errors. Understanding bias helps UX designers predict behaviors and improve user experiences. Examples of biases include negativity bias, peak-end rule, and anchoring bias. Biases influence memory, perceptions, and decisions. By recognizing these patterns, designers can create more intuitive, engaging, and effective interfaces.

articles.title: BOLT
articles.summary: Bolt enables one-click app creation with code generation and live previews. It supports frameworks like Next.js and Vue.js.

articles.title: IMPACTFUL B2B ADS, EXPLAINED
articles.summary: Get the inspiration and knowledge to create impactful ads with tips and practical examples for creating your perfect B2B ads and campaigns.

articles.title: BUILD POWERFUL APIS IN MINUTES
articles.summary: Buildship lets you visually create backend APIs, automate complex workflows, and quickly integrate with any tool or AI.

articles.title: OVERFLOW CLIP
articles.summary: The overflow: clip property offers a precise way to hide the limitations of overflow by restricting clipping to a single axis without creating scrollable containers. This method is useful for layouts that require single-direction clipping, scroll-driven animations, or decorative effects. It is supported by major browsers, with some variations in handling overflow-clip margin.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEM: FROM SCRATCH OR FROM THE SHELF?
articles.summary: Whether you choose between custom-built or off-the-shelf UI component libraries for design systems should depend on project needs, frameworks, and resources. Off-the-shelf options save time and resources but may lack flexibility. Styled components enable quick prototyping, while unstyled components offer customization. A blend of libraries or custom solutions is often used to balance efficiency and design flexibility.

articles.title: APPLE'S NEW DESIGN LANGUAGE
articles.summary: Apple's evolving design language reflects a shift towards immersive, spatially aware experiences that emphasize physicality and fluid motion. Inspired by Vision Pro, Apple's new design principles integrate depth, physics, and 3D interactions across the company's ecosystem. This approach signals a transformative era for UI that blends problem-solving with aesthetics to enhance user engagement and functionality.

articles.title: METHODS FOR RANDOM GRADIENTS
articles.summary: Random gradient images can be created using techniques like Perlin noise for smooth effects or SVG radial gradients for dynamic performance.

articles.title: CHATGPT WAS TASKED WITH DESIGNING A WEBSITE. THE RESULT WAS AS MANIPULATIVE AS YOU'D EXPECT
articles.summary: Generative AI often integrates deceptive design patterns into web pages, raising ethical concerns.

articles.title: YOU'RE NOT GETTING ENOUGH CREDIT FOR YOUR DESIGN WORK
articles.summary: Emphasizing design's measurable contributions to behavior, attitudes, and emotions helps align with business goals and foster credit.
